subject: [PATCH v2 14/14] Documentation/userfaultfd: document RWP working set tracking

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mike Rapoport suggested replacing 'VMM' with 'Callers' in the documentation to make it more accurate, as RWP is not limited to Virtual Machine Monitors (VMMs) and will have other use-cases.
reviews.raw_body:
Lets s/VMM/Callers/.
Although RWP is designed for VMMs, it's not limited to them and I expect
other use-cases will be coming along.

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's userfaultfd feature, specifically covering read-write protection (RWP) working set tracking. It explains the sync and async fault models, UFFDIO_RWPROTECT semantics, and UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC. The documentation also covers a typical workflow for VMM working-set-tracking from detection to eviction and back to async mode.

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's userfaultfd feature, specifically covering read-write protection (RWP) working set tracking. It explains the sync and async fault models, UFFDIO_RWPROTECT semantics, and UFFD_FEATURE_RWP_ASYNC. The documentation also covers a typical workflow for VMM working-set-tracking from detection to eviction and back to async mode.
